duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: rentalWallet field omitted from _deriveRentalOrderHash abi.encode, enabling order hash collision and wallet substitution in stopRent
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same EIP-712 encoding defect in Signer.sol _deriveRentalOrderHash where rentalWallet is skipped between order.renter and order.startTimestamp, enabling cross-wallet hash collisions during stopRent. ECON-H001 selected for CRITICAL severity, detailed attack economics, and comprehensive multi-path impact analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: orderType and emittedExtraData fields omitted from _deriveOrderMetadataHash, allowing fulfiller to alter order semantics without invalidating zoneHash
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All target the same encoding gap in Signer.sol _deriveOrderMetadataHash where metadata.orderType and metadata.emittedExtraData are absent from abi.encode despite being in the EIP-712 type string. ECON2-H003 focuses on emittedExtraData event spoofing specifically but shares the identical root cause. ECON-H002 selected for comprehensive coverage of both fields and attack economics.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Guard._checkTransaction uses a fixed selector blocklist that allows non-standard NFT transfer/approval functions (e.g., EIP-4494 permit) to pass through unblocked
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same Guard blocklist incompleteness in _checkTransaction where unrecognized selectors pass through the if/else chain, enabling NFT theft via permit() or custom transfer functions. Note: first occurrence of TECH2-H003 ('Guard Bypass via Non-Standard NFT Transfer Functions') is in this group. ECON-H004 selected for CRITICAL severity, detailed selector analysis, and comprehensive economics.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: EIP-712 _RENTAL_ORDER_TYPEHASH computed using abi.encode instead of abi.encodePacked for type string concatenation
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All target the same abi.encode vs abi.encodePacked inconsistency in Signer._deriveRentalTypehashes for rentalOrderTypeHash. Note: first occurrence of TECH2-H006 ('Incorrect EIP-712 Encoding for Type Hashes and Dynamic Bytes') also covers the hook hash issue but is primarily about the type hash bug. TECH-H004 selected for most focused technical analysis and clear evidence of internal inconsistency with rentPayloadTypeHash.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: EIP-712 _deriveHookHash passes hook.extraData directly to abi.encode instead of keccak256(hook.extraData) as required for dynamic bytes
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same EIP-712 violation in Signer._deriveHookHash where dynamic bytes extraData is not hashed per spec. TECH-H005 selected for more precise line references and clearer technical explanation.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Guard.checkTransaction reverts on data.length < 4, blocking pure ETH transfers and trapping ETH in rental Safes
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the identical data.length < 4 revert in Guard.checkTransaction that prevents ETH sends from rental Safes. TECH-H006 selected for more detailed analysis of the ETH trapping mechanism.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: CEI violation in Stop.stopRent/stopRentBatch: external calls (hooks, NFT safeTransferFrom callbacks, ERC20 transfers) execute before STORE.removeRentals, enabling reentrancy
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same CEI violation in Stop.sol where external calls occur before storage removal. Includes both stopRent reentrancy (TECH-H007, ECON-H007, OPS-H010, TECH2-H004, OPS2-H003) and stopRentBatch widened window (TECH2-H007, ECON-H015, ECON2-H011) which share the identical root code defect. Note: first occurrence of TECH2-H004 ('Reentrancy in stopRent') and first occurrence of TECH2-H007 ('stopRentBatch Widens Reentrancy') are in this group. ECON-H007 selected for HIGH severity, comprehensive coverage of both single and batch scenarios, and detailed cross-order manipulation analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Hook status bitmap change (onStop bit disabled) after rental creation causes _removeHooks to revert, permanently locking rental assets
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same defect: _removeHooks checks current STORE.hookOnStop(target) status rather than status at creation time, so a post-creation hook status change makes stopRent permanently revert. ECON2-H009 selected for CRITICAL severity and detailed attack economics showing permanent asset lock.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Whitelisted delegatecall targets (e.g., MultiSend) can bypass Guard entirely, execute arbitrary internal calls including NFT transfers and Safe storage overwrites; empty checkAfterExecution provides no post-execution verification
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All target the same vulnerability: Guard allows delegatecall to whitelisted addresses without inspecting internal operations, and checkAfterExecution is empty. TECH-H009/ECON-H008 emphasize the empty checkAfterExecution angle; TECH-H013/ECON-H016/ECON2-H004/OPS2-H006 emphasize MultiSend batching; all share the same root cause and fix. ECON-H005 selected for CRITICAL severity, most comprehensive scenario covering both internal call bypass and storage overwrite, with detailed economics.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Active hook for target contract completely bypasses Guard._checkTransaction via mutually exclusive if/else branch, delegating all security to potentially incomplete hook
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same code pattern: Guard.checkTransaction's if/else structure forwards to hook when active and skips _checkTransaction entirely. Note: first occurrence of TECH2-H008 ('Active Hooks Completely Bypass Guard') is in this group. ECON-H003 selected for CRITICAL severity, clearest explanation of the mutually exclusive branch pattern, and detailed economics.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Immutable EIP-712 domain separator does not check block.chainid at runtime, enabling cross-chain signature replay after chain fork
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the identical defect: _DOMAIN_SEPARATOR is immutable and _CHAIN_ID is stored but never compared to block.chainid in _recoverSignerFromPayload. ECON-H011 selected for HIGH severity with detailed attack economics and clear explanation of the fork replay mechanism.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Pro-rata calculation overflow in PaymentEscrow._calculatePaymentProRata blocks early settlement of PAY orders
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All target the same arithmetic overflow in (amount * elapsedTime) * 1000 in _calculatePaymentProRata. TECH-H018 also covers totalTime=0 edge case but shares the same primary function and defect. TECH-H012 selected for most focused analysis with clear overflow boundary calculations.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Factory.initializeRentalSafe is externally callable without access control, enabling Safe configuration manipulation or spoofed safe creation
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both target Factory.initializeRentalSafe's lack of access control. TECH-H014 focuses on creating lookalike safes; OPS2-H011 focuses on existing Safe owners calling it to alter guard/module config. Same root cause: unprotected external function. OPS2-H011 selected for CRITICAL severity and more detailed attack scenario.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Kernel policy deactivation uses live requestPermissions()/configureDependencies() calls rather than stored snapshot, leaving stale permissions if outputs change
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same Kernel lifecycle defect: _deactivatePolicy revokes permissions based on current requestPermissions() output, not activation-time snapshot. Note: second occurrence of TECH2-H006 ('Kernel policy deactivation may leave stale module permissions') is in this group. ECON2-H013 selected for HIGH severity and most detailed module upgrade scenario showing how stale permissions persist.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Kernel.executeAction allows setting executor/admin to address(0), permanently bricking governance
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the missing zero-address validation in ChangeExecutor/ChangeAdmin branches. Note: second occurrence of TECH2-H007 ('Kernel can be permanently bricked') is in this group. TECH-H016 selected for clearer investigation rationale.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Storage.addRentalSafe lacks duplicate/zero-address/contract-type validation, enabling inconsistent safe registry
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both target the same missing validation in Storage.addRentalSafe. OPS-H015 selected for more detailed exploitation scenario showing how spoofed safe registration bypasses Create._isValidSafeOwner.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: PaymentEscrow accounting assumes nominal amounts equal actual received amounts; fee-on-transfer/rebasing/deflationary tokens cause insolvency, settlement failure, and skim underflow
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All stem from the same root cause: PaymentEscrow.increaseDeposit records nominal amount without verifying actual balance delta. Fee-on-transfer threats (ECON-H017, ECON2-H006, ECON2-H015, OPS2-H009, OPS2-H014, OPS-H014) and skim underflow threats (ECON-H020, OPS2-H010) plus combined threats (TECH2-H008 second occurrence) all share this accounting defect. Note: second occurrence of TECH2-H008 ('PaymentEscrow skim underflows') is in this group. OPS-H009 selected for broadest inventory_refs coverage (increaseDeposit, _settlePayment, _safeTransfer, skim) and clear operational analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: PAYEE orders skip all storage/escrow/hook processing in _rentFromZone, creating zero on-chain footprint and making them untrackable/unstoppable
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same conditional skip in Create._rentFromZone where PAYEE orders fall outside the isBaseOrder/isPayOrder block. Note: first occurrence of TECH2-H005 ('PAYEE Orders Evade Storage Tracking') and second occurrence of TECH2-H003 ('PAYEE order validation creates untracked rentals') are in this group. ECON-H009 selected for HIGH severity, detailed analysis of PAY/PAYEE pairing failure, and economics.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: External hook's onStop can be manipulated to revert, permanently preventing stopRent and locking rental assets
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same vulnerability: stopRent's dependency on IHook(target).onStop() success allows a renter to manipulate hook state to cause permanent revert. Distinct from DUP-008 which is about admin-driven hook STATUS changes. ECON2-H005 selected for more detailed economics and clearer renter-manipulation scenario.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Stop.stopRent executes external interactions (hooks, delegatecall reclaim, settlement) before verifying order existence in Storage.removeRentals, enabling fake order exploitation
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same deferred existence check: stopRent performs external calls before STORE.removeRentals which is the first actual order existence verification. Note: second occurrence of TECH2-H005 ('Stop.stopRent can be triggered with fake RentalOrder') is in this group. OPS-H013 selected for CRITICAL severity and more detailed analysis of Safe module call atomicity implications.
